title: Quiz: Entanglement as a Quantum Resource
description: Test your understanding of the resource theory of entanglement, LOCC operations, and entanglement measures

questions.question: In the resource theory of entanglement, what are the free states?

options.text: Separable states ρ = Σᵢ pᵢ ρᵢᴬ ⊗ ρᵢᴮ

questions.explanation: The free states in entanglement theory are separable states - those that can be written as convex combinations of product states. These can be prepared using only LOCC (the free operations) without any pre-existing entanglement.

questions.question: According to Nielsen's majorization theorem, which transformation is possible under LOCC?

options.text: Any pure entangled state can be transformed to a Bell state
A Bell state can be transformed to any less entangled pure state
Schmidt coefficients must increase under LOCC
Entanglement entropy must increase under LOCC

questions.explanation: Nielsen's theorem states that |ψ⟩ → |φ⟩ is possible under LOCC iff the Schmidt coefficients of ψ are majorized by those of φ. The Bell state has uniform coefficients (1/√2, 1/√2), which majorizes any other distribution, so it can be transformed to any less entangled state.

questions.question: What is the entanglement entropy of a maximally entangled state of two qubits (Bell state)?
questions.explanation: A Bell state has Schmidt coefficients (1/2, 1/2). The entanglement entropy is S = -Σλᵢlog₂(λᵢ) = -2(1/2)log₂(1/2) = 1 ebit. This is the maximum for two qubits.

questions.question: A bound entangled state is characterized by:

options.text: Being entangled but having zero distillable entanglement

questions.explanation: Bound entangled states are entangled (not separable) but have zero distillable entanglement - no pure entanglement can be extracted from them using LOCC. They represent 'locked' quantum correlations.

questions.question: For mixed states, the entanglement cost E_C(ρ) always equals the distillable entanglement D(ρ).
questions.explanation: False. For mixed states, generally E_C(ρ) > D(ρ), meaning entanglement manipulation is irreversible. The gap E_C - D represents entropy produced during manipulation. Only for pure states are they equal.

questions.question: The negativity N(ρ) of a bipartite state measures:

options.text: The sum of all eigenvalues of ρ
The sum of absolute values of negative eigenvalues of the partial transpose
The distance to the nearest separable state
The minimum over all entanglement witnesses

questions.explanation: Negativity is defined as N(ρ) = (||ρᵀᴮ||₁ - 1)/2, which equals the sum of absolute values of negative eigenvalues of the partial transpose. It quantifies how much the PPT criterion is violated.

questions.question: What operational task requires exactly 1 ebit of entanglement?
questions.explanation: Quantum teleportation uses 1 ebit (a Bell state) plus 2 classical bits to transmit 1 qubit. This is the operational definition of 1 ebit - the entanglement required for teleportation.

questions.question: Which statements about LOCC operations are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.text: LOCC cannot create entanglement from separable states
All separable operations can be implemented by LOCC
Entanglement measures are non-increasing under LOCC
LOCC includes local unitaries and classical communication

questions.explanation: (a) Correct: LOCC preserves the set of separable states. (b) Incorrect: Some separable operations (preserving separable states) cannot be implemented by LOCC. (c) Correct: This is the definition of an entanglement monotone. (d) Correct: LOCC consists of local quantum operations and classical communication.
